revision.text:
'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には、記録があり長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20ｍの御神灯が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火。境内に爆竹音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞します。また手筒花火や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒します。なお4月に行われる「本祭」と8月に行われる「夏」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指します。
== 本祭 == [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[犬山線]]に乗車。「手力駅」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == 出場する町内が本祭の半分の復興・観光用の祭り。迫力は本祭さながら。 *開催日 毎年8月第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てちからのひまつり}} [[Category:岐阜県の祭り]] [[Category:岐阜市]]

'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には記録があり、長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20ｍの御神灯が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火。境内に爆竹音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞します。また手筒花火や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒します。なお4月に行われる「本祭」と8月に行われる「夏」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指します。
== 本祭 == [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[犬山線]]に乗車。「手力駅」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == 出場する町内が本祭の半分の復興・観光用の祭り。迫力は本祭さながら。 *開催日 毎年8月第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てちからのひまつり}} [[Category:岐阜県の祭り]] [[Category:岐阜市]]

'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には記録があり、長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20mの御神灯が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火。境内に爆竹音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞する。また手筒花火や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒させる。なお4月に行われる「'''本祭'''」と8月に行われる「'''夏'''」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指す。
== 本祭 == [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == 出場する町内が本祭の半分の復興・観光用の祭り。迫力は本祭さながら。本際では行われない通常の花火を地面で爆発させる「地割れ花火」や和太鼓の演奏もある。 *開催日 毎年8月の第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]

== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20mの御神灯が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火。境内に爆竹音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞する。また手筒花火や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒させる。なお4月に行われる「'''本祭'''」と8月に行われる「'''夏'''」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指す。
== 本祭 == [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。

'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には記録があり、長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20mの御神灯が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火。境内に爆竹音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞する。また[[手筒花火]]や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒させる。なお4月に行われる「'''本祭'''」と8月に行われる「'''夏'''」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指す。
== 本祭 == [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == 出場する町内が本祭の半分の復興・観光用の祭りイベント。本祭では行われない通常の花火を地面で爆発させる「地割れ花火」や和太鼓の演奏もある。会場の長良川公園は河川敷にあるため堤防の斜面に座って祭りを観覧出来るので、視野が広く火祭りの全てを楽しむ事が出来る。それでも迫力は本祭さながら。裸男衆に手抜きは無い。 *開催日 毎年8月の第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てちからのひまつり}} [[Category:岐阜県の祭り]] [[Category:岐阜市]] [[Category:無形民俗文化財 (都道府県指定)]]

revision.comment: 県指定文化財に重要は付かない
revision.text:
[[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（滝花火の火の粉の中で乱舞する神輿）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（火を噴く神輿）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（手筒花火の火の粉の中で乱舞する裸男衆）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（地割れ花火）]]
'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には記録があり、長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>。
== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20mの[[御神灯]]が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火される。境内に[[爆竹]]音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞する。また[[手筒花火]]や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒させる。なお4月に行われる「'''本祭'''」と8月に行われる「'''夏'''」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指す。
== 本祭 == '''本祭'''は、[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭である。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == '''夏'''は、復興・観光用の祭りイベントである。出場する町内は本祭の半分である。本祭では行われない通常の花火を地面で爆発させる「地割れ花火」や和太鼓の演奏もある。会場の長良川公園は河川敷にあるため堤防の斜面に座って祭りを観覧出来るので、視野が広く火祭りの全てを楽しむ事が出来る。それでも迫力は本祭さながらであり、裸男衆に手抜きは無い。 *開催日 毎年8月の第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]

'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には記録があり、長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>。

== 本祭 == '''本祭'''は、[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭である。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == '''夏'''は、復興・観光用の祭りイベントである。出場する町内は本祭の半分である。本祭では行われない通常の花火を地面で爆発させる「地割れ花火」や和太鼓の演奏もある。会場の長良川公園は河川敷にあるため堤防の斜面に座って祭りを観覧出来るので、視野が広く火祭りの全てを楽しむ事が出来る。それでも迫力は本祭さながらであり、裸男衆に手抜きは無い。 *開催日 毎年8月の第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} *[http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てちからのひまつり}} [[Category:岐阜県の祭り]] [[Category:岐阜市の文化]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} (てぢからおじんじゃ) in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}}. * **Date:** Second Saturday of April each year * **Location:** {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * **Access:** From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * **Date:** Second Sunday of August each year * **Location:** {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * **Access:** From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} (てぢからおじんじゃ) in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} (てぢからおじんじゃ) in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}

'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

[[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（滝花火の火の粉の中で乱舞する神輿）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（火を噴く神輿）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（手筒花火の火の粉の中で乱舞する裸男衆）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（地割れ花火）]]
'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には記録があり、長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>。
== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20mの[[御神灯]]が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火される。境内に[[爆竹]]音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞する。また[[手筒花火]]や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒させる。なお4月に行われる「'''本祭'''」と8月に行われる「'''夏'''」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指す。
元は[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の秋の例祭（大祭）で行われていたが、[[1960年]]（昭和35年）に例祭と火祭を分離。火祭を4月5日の[[岐阜まつり]]の日に開催とする<ref name="栞">手力雄神社の栞</ref>。[[1994年]]（平成6年）より4月第2土曜日に変更<ref name="栞"/>。
== 本祭 == '''本祭'''は、[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭である。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == '''夏'''は、復興・観光用の祭りイベントである。出場する町内は本祭の半分である。本祭では行われない通常の花火を地面で爆発させる「地割れ花火」や和太鼓の演奏もある。会場の長良川公園は河川敷にあるため堤防の斜面に座って祭りを観覧出来るので、視野が広く火祭りの全てを楽しむ事が出来る。それでも迫力は本祭さながらであり、裸男衆に手抜きは無い。 *開催日 毎年8月の第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} *[http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てちからのひまつり}} [[Category:岐阜県の祭り]] [[Category:岐阜市の文化]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.

== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.

== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}

Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.

[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|12=draft}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火|12=draft}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|12=draft}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|12=draft}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線|12=draft}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅|12=draft}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=draft}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車|12=draft}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:手力の火祭 up to rev 103818114
revision.text:
[[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（滝花火の火の粉の中で乱舞する神輿）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（火を噴く神輿）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（手筒花火の火の粉の中で乱舞する裸男衆）]] [[画像:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|手力の火祭・夏（地割れ花火）]]
'''手力の火祭'''（てぢからのひまつり）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（てぢからおじんじゃ）で行われる[[火祭り]]。始まった時期は不明ではあるが明和年間（1760年代）には記録があり、長く続く岐阜市を代表する伝統行事で岐阜県無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>。
== 概要 == 境内に設けられた高さ20mの[[御神灯]]が次々と点灯された後、滝花火に点火される。境内に[[爆竹]]音が響き渡り、激しい火の粉が降り注ぐ中、花火が仕掛けられた神輿を裸男たちが担ぎ乱舞する。また[[手筒花火]]や、仕掛け花火が一斉に火を噴く山焼きなど全てが見物客を圧倒させる。なお4月に行われる「'''本祭'''」と8月に行われる「'''夏'''」（祭りの復興・観光用）があるが通常は「本祭」の事を指す。
元は[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の秋の例祭（大祭）で行われていたが、[[1960年]]（昭和35年）に例祭と火祭を分離。火祭を4月5日の[[岐阜まつり]]の日に開催とする<ref name="栞">手力雄神社の栞</ref>。[[1994年]]（平成6年）より4月第2土曜日に変更<ref name="栞"/>。
== 本祭 == '''本祭'''は、[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭である。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == '''夏'''は、復興・観光用の祭りイベントである。出場する町内は本祭の半分である。本祭では行われない通常の花火を地面で爆発させる「地割れ花火」や和太鼓の演奏もある。会場の長良川公園は河川敷にあるため堤防の斜面に座って祭りを観覧出来るので、視野が広く火祭りの全てを楽しむ事が出来る。それでも迫力は本祭さながらであり、裸男衆に手抜きは無い。 *開催日 毎年8月の第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} *[http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てちからのひまつり}} [[Category:岐阜県の祭り]] [[Category:岐阜市の文化]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

元は[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の秋の例祭（大祭）で行われていたが、[[1960年]]（昭和35年）に例祭と火祭を分離。火祭を4月5日の[[岐阜まつり]]の日に開催とする<ref name="栞">手力雄神社の栞</ref>。[[1994年]]（平成6年）より4月第2土曜日に変更<ref name="栞"/>。
== 本祭 == '''本祭'''は、[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]の春の大祭である。 *開催日 毎年4月の第2土曜日 *場所 [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]]（岐阜県岐阜市蔵前6丁目8-22） *アクセス [[名鉄岐阜駅]]から[[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]]に乗車。「[[手力駅]]」にて下車し徒歩5分。
== 夏 == '''夏'''は、復興・観光用の祭りイベントである。出場する町内は本祭の半分である。本祭では行われない通常の花火を地面で爆発させる「地割れ花火」や和太鼓の演奏もある。会場の長良川公園は河川敷にあるため堤防の斜面に座って祭りを観覧出来るので、視野が広く火祭りの全てを楽しむ事が出来る。それでも迫力は本祭さながらであり、裸男衆に手抜きは無い。 *開催日 毎年8月の第2日曜日 *場所 [[長良川公園]] *アクセス [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] JR[[岐阜駅]]または[[名鉄岐阜駅]]から「高富」または「三田洞団地」行きに乗車（約20分）。「鵜飼屋」にて下車し徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|手力雄神社]] *[[火祭り]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} *[http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm 手力の火祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てちからのひまつり}} [[Category:岐阜県の祭り]] [[Category:岐阜市の文化]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
[[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|lt=Fire Festival|lt=Fire festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fire festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Tajikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|qq=}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|handheld fireworks|ja|手筒花火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/handheld fireworks|qq=}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tajikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine|qq=}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Gifu Station|qq=}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|ja|名鉄各務原線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|qq=}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|ja|手力駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikara Station|qq=}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|qq=}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Gifu Station|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tajikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Festival|lt=Fire Festival|lt=Fire festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire festival|qq=}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|qq=}} * '''Access:''' From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take a [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>

The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|handheld fireworks]], ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
