'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held at [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|handheld fireworks]], ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]]. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Tajikarao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496923}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take the [[:en:Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line]] to [[:en:Tejikara Station|Tejikara Station]], then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|qq=|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take a [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496921]] Wikidata name: Tejikara Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held at [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|handheld fireworks]], ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]]. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|1=Tajikarao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496923}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take the [[:en:Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line]] to [[:en:Tejikara Station|Tejikara Station]], then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take a [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]]
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496921]] Wikidata name: Tejikara Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held at [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|handheld fireworks]], ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]]. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|1=Tajikarao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496923}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take the [[:en:Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line]] to [[:en:Tejikara Station|Tejikara Station]], then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take a [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]]
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take a [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496921]] Wikidata name: Tejikara Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held at [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|handheld fireworks]], ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]]. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|1=Tajikarao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496923}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take the [[:en:Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line]] to [[:en:Tejikara Station|Tejikara Station]], then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take a [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]]
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496921]] Wikidata name: Tejikara Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held at [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include [[:en:Tezutsu-hanabi|handheld fireworks]], ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]]. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|1=Tajikarao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496923}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take the [[:en:Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line]] to [[:en:Tejikara Station|Tejikara Station]], then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR [[:en:Gifu Station|Gifu Station]] or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]], take a [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Tejikarao Shrine|Tajikarao Shrine]] * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]]
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]

Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|en|Gifu Station|WD=Q1144885|lt=Gifu Station}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tejikara Fire Festival]][[ja:手力の火祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496921]] Wikidata name: Tejikara Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 3.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi dancing amidst waterfall fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 1.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Mikoshi emitting flames)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 2.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Naked men dancing amidst handheld fireworks)]] [[File:Tedikara no himaturi natu 4.JPG|thumb|240px|Tejikara Fire Festival - Summer (Ground-splitting fireworks)]]
'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|en|Tejikarao Shrine|WD=Q3517214|lt=Tajikarao Shrine}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|Tezutsu-hanabi|en|Tezutsu-hanabi|WD=Q11497105|lt=handheld fireworks}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|en|Tejikarao Shrine|WD=Q3517214|lt=Tajikarao Shrine}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|1=Tajikarao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496923}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|en|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|WD=Q6810415|lt=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|en|Tejikara Station|WD=Q7695230|lt=Tejikara Station}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Nagara River Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|en|Gifu Station|WD=Q1144885|lt=Gifu Station}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|en|Tejikarao Shrine|WD=Q3517214|lt=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}}

'''Tejikara Fire Festival''' (手力の火祭, Tejikara no Himatsuri) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival|1=Fire Festival}} held at {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|en|Tejikarao Shrine|WD=Q3517214|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|1=Tejikarao Shrine}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. While its origins are unclear, records from the Meiwa era (1760s) indicate its long-standing history. It is a traditional event representative of Gifu City and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=手力雄神社火祭り |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>
== Overview == After the shrine's 20-meter-high {{ill|sacred lantern|ja|御神灯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is successively lit, a waterfall firework display is ignited. The sound of firecrackers reverberates through the shrine grounds, while fiery sparks rain down. Amidst the spectacular firework displays, a mikoshi (portable shrine) rigged with fireworks is carried and danced around by naked men. Highlights include {{ill|Tezutsu-hanabi|en|Tezutsu-hanabi|WD=Q11497105|lt=handheld fireworks|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, ground-level firework explosions, and mountain-burning fire displays that leave spectators in awe. There are two events: the "'''Main Festival'''," held in April, and "'''Summer'''," a revived version for tourism purposes held in August. Typically, the term "Main Festival" refers to the April event.
== Main Festival == The '''Main Festival''' is the spring grand festival of {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|en|Tejikarao Shrine|WD=Q3517214|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|1=Tejikarao Shrine}}. * '''Date:''' Second Saturday of April each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|1=Tajikarao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496923}} (6-8-22 Kuramae, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture) * '''Access:''' From {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}, take the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|en|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|WD=Q6810415|lt=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|1=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line}} to {{ill|Tejikara Station|en|Tejikara Station|WD=Q7695230|lt=Tejikara Station|1=Tejikara Station}}, then walk 5 minutes.
== Summer == The '''Summer''' event is a tourism-oriented revival of the festival. Participating neighborhood associations are fewer than in the Main Festival. Unique elements include ground-splitting fireworks, which explode on the ground, and taiko drum performances not featured in the Main Festival. Held at Nagara River Park, spectators can sit on the levee slopes to enjoy a wide view of the festival. Despite being a tourism event, the energy and dedication of the naked men carrying the mikoshi rival that of the Main Festival. * '''Date:''' Second Sunday of August each year * '''Location:''' {{ill|Nagara River Park|ja|長良川公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara River Park|1=Q11653625|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653625}} * '''Access:''' From JR {{ill|Gifu Station|en|Gifu Station|WD=Q1144885|lt=Gifu Station|1=Gifu Station}} or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|WD=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}, take a {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} heading to "Takami" or "Sandado Danchi" (approx. 20 minutes). Get off at "Ukaiya" and walk 3 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tejikarao Shrine|en|Tejikarao Shrine|WD=Q3517214|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|1=Tejikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival|1=Fire Festival}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline|Tejikara Fire Festival}} * [http://noishiki.com/Himatsuri/Himatsuri.htm Tejikara Fire Festival]
